headline: RM2.1 billion rural road programme: 40% of roads failed quality inspection
context: Forty percent of roads completed under the RM2.1 billion Program Jalan Perhubungan Luar Bandar failed quality inspections within two years of completion, requiring costly repairs.

cards.big: 40% of RM2.1 billion rural roads failed quality inspection within 2 years
cards.sub: Nearly half of new rural roads already crumbling before warranty periods expire

cards.big: JKR specification PWD-203 sets minimum road durability standards for rural roads
cards.sub: Standards exist on paper but enforcement relies on the same agency that approved construction

cards.big: Failed roads require RM340 million in repairs — 16% of original programme cost
cards.sub: Poor quality construction creates a cycle of building, failing, and rebuilding with public money

cards.big: 67% of failed-quality contracts were awarded to the same 12 companies
cards.sub: Repeat offenders continue receiving government contracts despite documented failures

cards.big: Building roads that fail in 2 years isn't development — it's a transfer of public money for substandard work
Should contractors who fail quality inspections be permanently blacklisted from government projects?
